summary: George Russell's controversial pole position sets up a fierce battle with Charles Leclerc and the Ferrari challenge at a circuit where Mercedes has seized momentum through Friday practice pace. Oscar Piastri and McLaren lurk as genuine contenders to disrupt the leading teams, while extreme heat conditions threaten to shuffle the grid's hierarchy and force aggressive tire management decisions. A victory here could prove decisive in the constructors' championship fight, with Mercedes and Ferrari locked in combat just as McLaren emerges as a genuine third force.
